title: Determine Operator Station And Tipper Status From Stacklight Indications
summary: Use the documented operator station stacklight colors, flashing patterns, and horn patterns to determine the current status of the corresponding tipper and station.
when_to_use: Use this reference when you need to identify the current documented status of an operator station or corresponding tipper by observing the station stacklights and any associated horn pattern.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer corrective actions beyond the documented status meanings.
Do not use this runbook when the observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the operator station stacklights and horn pattern, compare the indication to the documented mapping, and record or communicate the matching status meaning.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to observing, matching, and recording status indications from the source.
Do not infer corrective actions beyond the documented status meanings.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the operator station stacklights
Ability to hear horn alarms at the station

system_preconditions: The operator station stacklights are visible to the user.
Any horn alarm at the station can be heard by the user.
external_references: OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 3.2.4 Operator Station Stacklights

visual_references.description: Photo of the operator station showing the physical layout and the stacklights mounted on the top of the frame on either side.

steps.title: Locate the operator station stacklights
steps.purpose: Identify the correct visual indicators used to determine station and tipper status.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station and locate the two stacklights at the top of either side of the frame.
steps.expected_result: The two operator station stacklights are identified.
steps.healthy_condition: Both stacklights are visible at the top of either side of the operator station frame.
steps.failure_condition: The stacklights cannot be located or are not visible from the operator position.

actions.label: Locate stacklights
actions.exact_action: Go to the operator station and locate the two stacklights at the top of either side of the frame.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the operator station layout and the stacklights mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the stacklights cannot be located or observed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The operator stations each have two stacklights located at the top of either side of the frame. The stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station.
Figure 3-1 appears on page 24 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_3_2_operator_station.

steps.title: Determine whether the indication is solid or flashing
steps.purpose: Differentiate between steady and flashing indications before matching the status.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the active indication is solid or flashing.
steps.expected_result: The indication is classified as either solid or flashing.
steps.healthy_condition: The light behavior can be clearly observed as solid or flashing.
steps.failure_condition: The light behavior cannot be clearly determined.

actions.label: Observe light behavior
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the active indication is solid or flashing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station reference image to confirm the physical location of the stacklights while observing their behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed light behavior cannot be determined.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Stacklight indications include both solid and flashing patterns for different statuses.

steps.title: Identify the displayed color or color sequence
steps.purpose: Capture the exact color or multi-color pattern needed to match the documented status.
steps.instruction: Identify the displayed color or color sequence, including Blue, Green, Red, White, Yellow, or the documented multi-color combinations.
steps.expected_result: The observed color or color sequence is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: A single color or documented multi-color sequence can be clearly recognized.
steps.failure_condition: The color or sequence cannot be clearly identified.

actions.label: Identify color pattern
actions.exact_action: Identify the displayed color or color sequence, including Blue, Green, Red, White, Yellow, or the documented multi-color combinations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the station photo to orient yourself to the stacklight location while identifying the active color or sequence.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed color or sequence does not match a documented indication.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Solid Blue: Tipper is paused; Solid Green: Tipping in process; Solid Red: Estop is activated; Solid White: AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green: RMS error; Flashing Blue: Tipper is disabled; Flashing Green: Tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red: Tipper is faulted; Flashing White: SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow: AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm: Sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm: Bagout is complete.

steps.title: Listen for any horn pattern
steps.purpose: Capture horn behavior that is required to distinguish certain documented statuses.
steps.instruction: Listen for any horn pattern and note whether there is a long horn alarm or an intermittent horn alarm when present.
steps.expected_result: Any associated horn pattern is identified and noted.
steps.healthy_condition: The presence or absence of a horn, and its pattern when present, can be determined.
steps.failure_condition: The horn pattern cannot be heard or distinguished.

actions.label: Listen for horn pattern
actions.exact_action: Listen for any horn pattern and note whether there is a long horn alarm or an intermittent horn alarm when present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station reference image to confirm you are at the correct station while listening for any horn associated with the stacklight indication.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a horn pattern is needed to interpret the indication but cannot be heard or distinguished.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm: Sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm: Bagout is complete.

steps.title: Match the observed indication to the documented status mapping
steps.purpose: Translate the observed light and horn pattern into the documented station or tipper status.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed indication to the documented mapping: Solid Blue = tipper is paused; Solid Green = tipping in process; Solid Red = Estop is activated; Solid White = AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green = RMS error; Flashing Blue = tipper is disabled; Flashing Green = tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red = tipper is faulted; Flashing White = SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow = AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm = sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm = bagout is complete.
steps.expected_result: A documented status meaning is matched to the observed indication.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed light and horn pattern matches one documented status meaning from the source.
steps.failure_condition: The observed light and horn pattern does not match any documented status meaning from the source.

actions.label: Compare to mapping
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed indication to the documented mapping: Solid Blue = tipper is paused; Solid Green = tipping in process; Solid Red = Estop is activated; Solid White = AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green = RMS error; Flashing Blue = tipper is disabled; Flashing Green = tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red = tipper is faulted; Flashing White = SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow = AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm = sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm = bagout is complete.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication in the source.
Stop if interpretation would require inferring corrective actions beyond the documented status meanings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Solid Blue: Tipper is paused; Solid Green: Tipping in process; Solid Red: Estop is activated; Solid White: AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green: RMS error; Flashing Blue: Tipper is disabled; Flashing Green: Tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red: Tipper is faulted; Flashing White: SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow: AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm: Sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm: Bagout is complete.

steps.title: Record or communicate the matched status
steps.purpose: Ensure the documented status meaning is retained or shared for operations awareness.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the documented status meaning that matches the observed indication.
steps.expected_result: The matched documented status is recorded or communicated.
steps.healthy_condition: The status meaning is clearly documented or communicated without adding unsupported interpretation.
steps.failure_condition: The status is not recorded or communicated, or unsupported interpretation is added.

actions.label: Record or communicate status
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the documented status meaning that matches the observed indication.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if no documented status meaning can be matched.
Stop if communication would require unsupported corrective guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station.

success_criteria: The user identifies the documented current status of the corresponding tipper or station from the observed stacklight and horn indication.
The matched status meaning is recorded or communicated without adding unsupported corrective action.
healthy_conditions: The operator station stacklights are visible and can be observed.
The active indication can be distinguished as solid or flashing.
The displayed color or documented multi-color sequence can be identified.
Any associated horn pattern can be heard and distinguished when present.
The observed indication matches one documented status meaning from the source.
failure_conditions: The stacklights cannot be located or observed.
The light behavior cannot be determined as solid or flashing.
The color or sequence cannot be identified.
A required horn pattern cannot be heard or distinguished.
The observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication in the source.
Do not infer corrective actions beyond the documented status meanings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station physical layout and stacklight locations at the top of either side of the frame.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The operator stations each have two stacklights located at the top of either side of the frame. The stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station.

Solid Blue: Tipper is paused; Solid Green: Tipping in process; Solid Red: Estop is activated; Solid White: AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green: RMS error; Flashing Blue: Tipper is disabled; Flashing Green: Tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red: Tipper is faulted; Flashing White: SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow: AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm: Sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm: Bagout is complete.

missing_details: The source provides status meanings only and does not provide corrective actions for any stacklight condition.
The source does not provide a time estimate for using this reference.
The source does not specify production stop or LOTO requirements for this reference activity.
No source-specific artifact in the packet directly shows the operator station stacklight indications themselves; the attached image is a station layout reference.
